---
title: "Glossary of GitHub Actions Workflow Keywords"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Glossary of GitHub Actions Workflow Keywords}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

This glossary lists keywords used in the GitHub Actions workflows and actions in this repository.

## Workflow and Action Keywords

- [`concurrency`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#concurrency): Controls the concurrency of workflow runs.
- [`env`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#env): Sets environment variables for jobs or steps.
- [`id`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#jobsjob_idstepsid): Sets an identifier for a step, used for referencing outputs.
- [`if`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#if): Condition to determine if a job or step should run.
- [`inputs`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#inputs): Defines input parameters for workflows or actions.
- [`jobs`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#jobs): Groups together all the jobs that run in the workflow.
- [`name`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#name): Sets the name of the workflow, job, or step.
- [`needs`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#jobsjob_idneeds): Specifies job dependencies.
- [`on`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#on): Specifies the events that trigger the workflow.
- [`outputs`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#jobsjob_idoutputs): Defines outputs for jobs or steps.
- [`run`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#jobsjob_idstepsrun): Runs a command-line script in a step.
- [`runs-on`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#jobsjob_idruns-on): Specifies the type of machine to run the job on.
- [`secrets`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#secrets): Defines secrets passed to workflows or actions.
- [`shell`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#jobsjob_idstepsshell): Specifies the shell to use for a `run` step.
- [`steps`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#jobsjob_idsteps): Lists the sequence of tasks to run as part of a job.
- [`uses`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#jobsjob_idstepsuses): Specifies an action to use in a step.
- [`with`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#jobsjob_idstepswith): Provides input parameters to an action.
- [`workflow_call`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/reusing-workflows#calling-a-reusable-workflow): Enables a workflow to be called by other workflows.
- [`workflow_dispatch`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/events-that-trigger-workflows#workflow_dispatch): Allows manual triggering of workflows with optional inputs.

---

For more details, see the [GitHub Actions Workflow Syntax documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions).
